# TorqueSpec

> Bolt torque specification database. Calculated from ISO 898-1, SAE J429, and VDI 2230 standards using T = K × D × F.

## Main Pages

- [Homepage](https://torquespec.org/): Search all bolt torque specifications
- [Metric Bolts](https://torquespec.org/metric/): ISO metric bolt torque chart — M3 to M64, classes 4.6–12.9
- [Imperial Bolts](https://torquespec.org/imperial/): SAE imperial bolt torque chart — #6 to 1-1/2", grades 2, 5, 8
- [Torque Calculator](https://torquespec.org/calculator/): Interactive bolt torque calculator with all unit conversions
- [About / Methodology](https://torquespec.org/about/): How we calculate — formula, standards, K-factor table, validation

## Metric Bolt Specifications

- [M10 Class 10.9](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-10.9/): 72.2 N·m / 53.3 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 12.9](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-12.9/): 84.4 N·m / 62.3 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 4.6](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-4.6/): 19.6 N·m / 14.5 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 4.8](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-4.8/): 27.0 N·m / 19.9 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 5.6](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-5.6/): 24.4 N·m / 18.0 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 5.8](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-5.8/): 33.1 N·m / 24.4 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 6.8](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-6.8/): 38.3 N·m / 28.2 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 8.8](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-8.8/): 50.5 N·m / 37.2 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M10 Class 9.8](https://torquespec.org/metric/m10-9.8/): 56.6 N·m / 41.7 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M12 Class 10.9](https://torquespec.org/metric/m12-10.9/): 125.9 N·m / 92.9 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M12 Class 12.9](https://torquespec.org/metric/m12-12.9/): 147.1 N·m / 108.5 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)
- [M12 Class 4.6](https://torquespec.org/metric/m12-4.6/): 34.1 N·m / 25.2 ft·lbs (dry, coarse thread)

- [#10" Grade 2](https://torquespec.org/imperial/no10-grade-2/): 2.3 ft·lbs / 3.1 N·m (dry, UNC thread)

- [5/8" Grade 8](https://torquespec.org/imperial/5-8-grade-8/): 211.9 ft·lbs / 287.3 N·m (dry, UNC thread)

- [7/16" Grade 5](https://torquespec.org/imperial/7-16-grade-5/): 49.4 ft·lbs / 67.0 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [7/16" Grade 8](https://torquespec.org/imperial/7-16-grade-8/): 69.8 ft·lbs / 94.6 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [7/8" Grade 2](https://torquespec.org/imperial/7-8-grade-2/): 166.8 ft·lbs / 226.2 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [7/8" Grade 5](https://torquespec.org/imperial/7-8-grade-5/): 429.5 ft·lbs / 582.3 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [7/8" Grade 8](https://torquespec.org/imperial/7-8-grade-8/): 606.4 ft·lbs / 822.2 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [9/16" Grade 2](https://torquespec.org/imperial/9-16-grade-2/): 70.4 ft·lbs / 95.4 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [9/16" Grade 5](https://torquespec.org/imperial/9-16-grade-5/): 108.8 ft·lbs / 147.5 N·m (dry, UNC thread)
- [9/16" Grade 8](https://torquespec.org/imperial/9-16-grade-8/): 153.6 ft·lbs / 208.3 N·m (dry, UNC thread)

## Data Coverage

- **Metric:** 25 sizes (M3–M64) × 9 property classes (4.6, 4.8, 5.6, 5.8, 6.8, 8.8, 9.8, 10.9, 12.9) = 210 combinations
- **Imperial:** 17 sizes (#6–1-1/2") × 3 grades (2, 5, 8) = 51 combinations
- **Surface conditions:** 6 per combination (dry K=0.20, oiled K=0.15, zinc plated K=0.18, waxed K=0.10, MoS₂ K=0.12, galvanized K=0.25)
- **Thread types:** Coarse + fine (metric), UNC + UNF (imperial)
- **Units:** N·m, ft·lbs, in·lbs, kgf·cm

## Methodology

All torque values are calculated, not copied. Formula: T = K × D × F, where F = 0.75 × proof_stress × stress_area. Proof stress from ISO 898-1 (metric) and SAE J429 (imperial). Stress areas from ISO 261/262 and ASME B1.1. K-factors from VDI 2230. Validated within ±2% of Portland Bolt, Fastenal, Bolt Depot, and Fractory reference tables.
